Package ru.nsu.kiryushin

Class BlockingList<T>

java.lang.Object
ru.nsu.kiryushin.BlockingList<T>
Type Parameters:
T - stored element type
public class BlockingList<T> extends Object
Thread-safe bounded list with blocking put and take operations.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    BlockingList(int capacity)
    Creates a blocking list with a fixed capacity.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Closes the list and wakes all waiting threads.
    boolean
    put(T item)
    Adds an element, waiting until space becomes available if needed.
    boolean
    put(T item, Runnable onWait)
    Adds an element, optionally invoking a callback once before the thread blocks.
    T
    take()
    Removes and returns the first available element.
    List<T>
    takeMultiple(int max)
    Removes up to max elements from the head of the list.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • BlockingList

      public BlockingList(int capacity)
      Creates a blocking list with a fixed capacity.
      Parameters:
      capacity - maximum number of elements stored at once

  • Method Details

    • put

      public boolean put(T item) throws InterruptedException
      Adds an element, waiting until space becomes available if needed.
      Parameters:
      item - element to add
      Returns:
      true if the element was added, false if the list was closed
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the current thread is interrupted while waiting

    • put

      public boolean put(T item, Runnable onWait) throws InterruptedException
      Adds an element, optionally invoking a callback once before the thread blocks.
      Parameters:
      item - element to add
      onWait - callback invoked once before waiting for free space
      Returns:
      true if the element was added, false if the list was closed
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the current thread is interrupted while waiting

    • take

      public T take() throws InterruptedException
      Removes and returns the first available element.
      Returns:
      the next element, or null if the list is closed and empty
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the current thread is interrupted while waiting

    • takeMultiple

      public List<T> takeMultiple(int max) throws InterruptedException
      Removes up to max elements from the head of the list.
      Parameters:
      max - maximum number of elements to return
      Returns:
      a list containing removed elements, or an empty list if the list is closed and empty
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the current thread is interrupted while waiting

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes the list and wakes all waiting threads.
